content: Hi, I'm Logan. I'm a physical therapist and I love hiking and archery in my free time. I'm actually planning a trip to Sedona with my wife, Emily, on October 15th.
Nice to meet you, Logan! Sedona is a beautiful place. What do you like to do when you're not working or hiking?
I'm really into knitting and chess. I find that knitting helps me relax and chess keeps my mind sharp. I've been playing chess with my friend, Michael, online for a few years now.
That's great! Knitting and chess are both wonderful hobbies. Have you ever thought about combining your physical therapy background with your love of hiking and outdoor activities?

Actually, I've been thinking about starting a blog or YouTube channel focused on outdoor fitness and injury prevention. I've been taking a part-time MBA course at the University of Phoenix to learn more about business and marketing.
That's a fantastic idea! There are so many people who could benefit from that kind of expertise. What inspired you to pursue an MBA?

I wanted to learn more about the business side of things and how to market myself as a physical therapist. I've been working with a business coach, David, who has been helping me develop a plan for my blog and YouTube channel.
That makes sense. It's always important to have a solid understanding of the business side of things. Do you have any favorite coffee spots in Phoenix where you like to study or work?
Yeah, I love going to Cartel Coffee Lab. They have a great atmosphere and excellent coffee. I usually meet my study group there on Sundays to work on our projects.
I'll have to check it out. What kind of archery do you enjoy? Are you more into target archery or bowhunting?

I'm actually more into target archery. I love the precision and focus required to hit the target. I've been practicing at the archery range in Glendale with my friend, Sarah, who is also into archery.
Target archery can be so meditative and rewarding. Maybe I can help you find some new archery ranges or clubs in the Phoenix area to try out?
